# Pull Request `{number}`: “`{title}`”

## Description

Describe the pull request’s motivation and what it achieves. If the commits’ messages are detailed
enough, copy–paste them here.

`{description}`

**Closes:** `{issue1}`, `{issue2}`

## Type of changes

What do the changes in the pull request do?

- [ ] ✨ They add new features.
- [ ] 🐞 They fix bugs.
- [ ] ♻️ They refactor code.
- [ ] ⚙️ They update configurations and/or dependencies.
- [ ] 📝 They add or updates documentation.

Do the changes break the current behavior?

- [ ] 💥 Yes, they do.
- [ ] 💤 No, they do not.

## Checklist

Review this checklist _before_ accepting the pull request.

### Code

- [ ] The code is properly formatted.
- [ ] New configurations are defined in dedicated files and not hardcoded.

### Testing

- [ ] New tests were written if new features were added or bugs were fixed.
- [ ] Existing tests were reviewed and updated if needed.
- [ ] The changes were tested together with the frontend running.
